summary: Small matching PR that completed two straightforward functions: renamed the symbol at 0x800263B4 to lbAudioAx_ObjFree and implemented ftCo_Attack100's fn_800DC044 as a tap-jump-threshold check on the fighter's left-stick Y input. The decomp.dev report credited 2 new matches and +96 bytes total.
what_changed: In config/GALE01/symbols.txt, replaced the placeholder symbol fn_800263B4 with the named function lbAudioAx_ObjFree at .text:0x800263B4, size 0x34.
Implemented bool fn_800DC044(Fighter_GObj* gobj) in ftCo_Attack100.c.
fn_800DC044 obtains the Fighter with GET_FIGHTER(gobj), compares fp->input.lstick.y against p_ftCommonData->tap_jump_threshold, returns true when the threshold is met, otherwise false.
Updated nearby ftCo_Attack100.h prototypes for fn_800DC014, fn_800DC044, and fn_800DC070 to include the parameter name gobj.
decomp.dev reported 2 new matches: lbAudioAx_ObjFree (+52 bytes) and fn_800DC044 (+44 bytes), bringing both from 0.00% to 100.00%.
smart_moves: Kept the fighter function implementation minimal and directly expressed the observed condition: left-stick vertical input greater than or equal to the common tap-jump threshold.
Used existing project idioms, notably Fighter_GObj*, GET_FIGHTER(gobj), Fighter* fp, and p_ftCommonData->tap_jump_threshold, rather than introducing new abstractions.
Renamed the audio symbol in the config when the function identity was known, improving symbol searchability without changing adjacent lbAudioAx entries.
Added parameter names in the header for local consistency with surrounding typed prototypes such as ftCo_CaptureWaitLw_Phys(Fighter_GObj* gobj).
decomp_lessons: Very small boolean helpers can often be matched cleanly by spelling out explicit true/false returns instead of relying on expression folding that may alter codegen.
For fighter input threshold checks, prefer the established pattern of GET_FIGHTER(gobj), reading fp->input.lstick fields, and comparing against p_ftCommonData constants.
When resolving a placeholder fn_ address to a domain name, update config/GALE01/symbols.txt so reports and future references use the named symbol.
Header cleanup can be paired with matching work when it simply adds parameter names and does not alter function types.
naming_conventions: Audio-library functions use the lbAudioAx_ prefix; this PR names 0x800263B4 as lbAudioAx_ObjFree.
Unidentified fighter functions remain in address-based fn_XXXXXXXX form, e.g. fn_800DC044, until a semantic name is established.
Fighter object parameters are named gobj in prototypes and implementations.
Local fighter pointers commonly use the name fp after GET_FIGHTER(gobj).
Common fighter data is accessed through p_ftCommonData, with fields such as tap_jump_threshold.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Matched fn_800DC044 as a compact threshold predicate: load Fighter from gobj, load fp->input.lstick.y, compare to p_ftCommonData->tap_jump_threshold, branch to true/false returns.
Used an explicit if block returning true and a final return false, which likely preserved the intended branch structure for this small boolean function.
Symbol-only rename of fn_800263B4 to lbAudioAx_ObjFree was enough for the lb/lbaudio_ax unit to report a new 52-byte match.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
Automated decomp.dev feedback reported GALE01 matched code unchanged in percentage at 54.48% but with +96 bytes and 2 new matches.
follow_up_queries: Find callers of fn_800DC044 to determine whether it should receive a semantic name related to tap jump or capture jump input.
Inspect neighboring functions fn_800DC014 and fn_800DC070 for similar simple input predicates or opportunities to replace address-based names.
Review lbAudioAx_ObjFree implementation or references to confirm whether additional lbAudioAx placeholder symbols can be named.
Search for other uses of p_ftCommonData->tap_jump_threshold to reuse matching patterns for tap-jump checks.
